title: NOC 82030 – Agricultural service contractors and farm supervisors | GTR Immigration
description: Agricultural service contractors and farm supervisors under NOC 82030 are the operational heartbeat of Canada's massive agri-food sector. As a TEER 2 occupation, these professionals bridge the gap between farm owners and laborers, coordinating complex activities ranging from livestock breeding to large-scale crop harvesting. In 2026, as Canada integrates "Smart Farming" technologies—including autonomous tractors and drone-based monitoring—these supervisors are essential for maintaining productivity and food security. For immigration, this role is a high-priority candidate for Express Entry Category-Based Selection (Agriculture) and the Saskatchewan Agriculture Talent Pathway.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category8 – Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupationsTEER Level2Major Group82 – Supervisors in natural resources, agriculture and related productionSub-Major Group820 – Supervisors in natural resources, agriculture and related productionMinor Group8203 – Contractors and supervisors, agriculture, horticulture and aquacultureNOC Code82030

duties: Supervise, coordinate, and schedule the activities of farm workers and livestock laborers.
Develop and oversee work schedules and procedures to ensure production targets are met.
Establish methods to maximize crop yields or livestock health and resolve operational problems.
Requisition materials and supplies and manage the maintenance of high-tech farm machinery.
Train workers in job duties, advanced safety procedures, and the operation of modern agricultural technology.
Maintain comprehensive production and quality control records for regulatory and business purposes.
Ensure strict adherence to provincial occupational health and safety (OHS) regulations.
May perform the same duties as workers supervised and manage specialized agricultural services.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Work Experience: Several years of experience in the specific type of farming supervised (e.g., grain, dairy, or fruit) are mandatory.
Education: A college diploma or university degree in agricultural science or a related field is increasingly preferred for modern corporate farm roles.
Technical Skills: Proficiency in digital farm management software and automated equipment is highly valued.
Strong leadership, communication, and the ability to work in outdoor environments are essential.
salary_prospects: Note: Total compensation often includes non-monetary benefits such as on-site housing, utility allowances, and vehicle usage.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. According to the Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS), the sector is experiencing a "Technological Re-skilling" phase. While total farm labor numbers remain stable, the complexity of supervisory roles is increasing. Retirements of senior farm staff will remain the primary driver of new openings through 2028.
labour_market_demand: The demand for agricultural supervisors is Very Good, particularly in Canada's "Breadbasket" provinces. In 2026, Saskatchewan, Alberta, and Ontario are facing critical shortages as many experienced supervisors transition into management or retirement. With the expansion of high-value exports and the rise of precision agriculture, Canadian farm operators are actively recruiting international talent with strong technical and leadership backgrounds.
Global Agri-Food Leadership: Lead crews in a country that is a top-tier global exporter of wheat, canola, and pulses.
Technological Innovation: Manage the implementation of "Precision Agriculture," using GPS-guided machinery and AI-driven soil analysis.
Competitive Compensation: As a TEER 2 supervisory role, salaries are significantly higher than entry-level farm work, often including housing and performance incentives.
Direct Path to PR: A high-priority "In-Demand" occupation for provincial nomination and specialized Express Entry draws.
pathways: Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Highly prioritized for candidates with experience in the "Agriculture and Agri-Food" category.
Saskatchewan Agriculture Talent Pathway: A specialized, high-priority route for supervisors with a job offer in the province.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Fast-track PR for those currently working in Alberta’s agricultural sector.
OINP Employer Job Offer (In-Demand Skills): A direct route for those with a job offer from an Ontario-based agricultural firm.
